scope: M1.2-1 baseline freeze and bad case clustering

privacy.note: Bad cases are referenced only by eval query id, case id, metrics, labels, and short sanitized notes.

current.reason: LeCaRDv2 candidate case texts were not found; current rerank cannot be evaluated against qrels.

blockedItems.evidence: Current rerank cannot be evaluated against LeCaRDv2 qrels.
Preflight found no readable docs at the configured LeCaRDv2 candidate path.

blockedItems.reason: current NDCG@10 and Top10 hit rate are below baseline or threshold
blockedItems.evidence: current NDCG@10=0.1248 < baseline 0.134; current Top10 hit rate=0.44 < threshold 0.60 and baseline 0.48.

blockedItems.reason: latest product bad cases all include DEPENDENCY_UNAVAILABLE
blockedItems.evidence: 25/25 product bad cases include DEPENDENCY_UNAVAILABLE; performance smoke saw 5/5 embedding timeouts and BM25 fallback.
DB reachable=false; blocks future durable features but not internal basic search.
Final verification allows internal basic search only.

clusters.label: RECALL_MISS
clusters.sanitizedNote: Relevant case ids did not appear in current top10 for these bad cases, but latest run also had dependency degradation.

clusters.label: QUERY_UNDERSTANDING_GAP
clusters.sanitizedNote: Existing reports do not contain a direct query-understanding label. Future annotation should distinguish user wording loss from recall or rerank errors.

clusters.label: RERANK_OVERWEIGHT
clusters.sanitizedNote: All latest bad cases had key-paragraph mismatch labels, but no direct RERANK_OVERWEIGHT label exists. Treat as a ranking hypothesis, not as a tuning instruction.

clusters.label: CAUSE_BIAS
clusters.sanitizedNote: No existing latest bad case is labeled as case-cause overweight or cause bias.

clusters.label: KEY_PARAGRAPH_MISS
clusters.sanitizedNote: Existing note says non-relevant current top results received key-paragraph feature score; no raw case text included.

clusters.label: DATASET_MISMATCH
clusters.scope: rerank product smoke and standard eval comparability
clusters.sanitizedNote: Product retrieval returns JuDGE/Chroma/BM25 product case ids, while LeCaRDv2 qrels use candidate ids and no verified mapping exists.

clusters.label: DEPENDENCY_UNAVAILABLE
clusters.scope: latest product bad cases and rerank eval
clusters.sanitizedNote: Latest product eval bad cases all indicate dependency degradation; this blocks clean recall-vs-ranking attribution.

clusters.label: METRIC_REGRESSION
clusters.sanitizedNote: Only two latest bad cases have direct metric regression labels, but aggregate NDCG@10 and Top10 hit rate regressed.

topProblemDistribution.label: DEPENDENCY_UNAVAILABLE
topProblemDistribution.interpretation: Primary blocker for clean attribution in the frozen latest current run.

topProblemDistribution.label: KEY_PARAGRAPH_MISS
topProblemDistribution.interpretation: Strong product bad-case family, but latest run needs clean rerun before weight tuning.

topProblemDistribution.label: RECALL_MISS
topProblemDistribution.interpretation: Likely contributes to Top10 hit loss.

topProblemDistribution.label: DATASET_MISMATCH
topProblemDistribution.interpretation: Standard rerank/product qrels comparison remains non-comparable; scope is rerank product smoke, not product bad-case count.

topProblemDistribution.label: METRIC_REGRESSION
topProblemDistribution.interpretation: Direct latest per-query regression labels are fewer than aggregate metric regression, likely due to dependency overlay and label granularity.

topProblemDistribution.label: QUERY_UNDERSTANDING_GAP
topProblemDistribution.interpretation: Not supported by existing labels; requires future annotation fields.

topProblemDistribution.label: CAUSE_BIAS
topProblemDistribution.interpretation: Not supported by existing labels; requires future annotation fields.

annotationGaps.reason: Existing labels are multi-label and latest run overlays dependency issues on every bad case.
Needed to distinguish RECALL_MISS from ranking suppression.
Needed to separate CAUSE_BIAS from KEY_PARAGRAPH_MISS and other ranking issues.

conclusion.answer: The observed frozen current regression is primarily from evaluation comparability/runtime dependency contamination, not cleanly attributable to recall or rerank weight changes.

recallVsRankingVsEval.recall: Secondary evidence: 13/25 latest product bad cases are labeled RECALL_MISS.
recallVsRankingVsEval.ranking: Secondary hypothesis: 25/25 latest and prior product bad cases are labeled KEY_PARAGRAPH_MISMATCH, but direct RERANK_OVERWEIGHT evidence is missing.
recallVsRankingVsEval.evaluationScope: Primary current issue: standard rerank eval is blocked and latest product current run is dependency-degraded.

conclusion.enableWeightedRerankReason: Product current NDCG@10 and Top10 hit rate are below baseline or threshold; standard rerank eval is blocked; latest bad cases all include dependency degradation.
conclusion.allowEnterM1_2_2Reason: M1.2-2 is the appropriate next step because the evidence points to fixing comparable eval lines and clean reruns before algorithm changes.
